summary.title: Fix master CI note replay checks
summary.what: Stabilized mainline note replay ordering, adjusted the affected property test for sync auto-pin behavior, and added a focused golangci-lint configuration with small lint cleanups.
summary.why: Latest master CI was failing in build/test because note replay could apply same-timestamp merge and revert notes in the wrong order, and lint lacked a scoped configuration for the current codebase.
summary.user_goal: Investigate latest master check failures and fix them on a new conventional branch.

decisions.chose: Replay reachable main notes by main ancestry order with timestamp as a tie fallback.
decisions.rationale: Author timestamps can collide in property tests and real fast operations, while ancestry order reflects the actual main history.

decisions.chose: Enable govet, ineffassign, and staticcheck explicitly.
decisions.rationale: This matches high-signal CI linting without introducing broad historical errcheck churn.

rejected.alternative: Keep sorting notes only by author timestamp.
rejected.reason: It leaves replay nondeterministic when commits share timestamps.

rejected.alternative: Enable every golangci-lint default linter implicitly.
rejected.reason: That turns CI into broad cleanup unrelated to the master failure.

risks: Full race suite is slow locally; targeted property tests and quick-test passed on the rebased branch.

architectural_claims: Main note replay should follow main commit ancestry rather than relying solely on timestamps.
behavioral_changes: scanMainNotes now replays reachable notes in stable main-history order.
Reconcile idempotency property test disables sync auto-pin before explicitly testing manual pinning.
golangci-lint now runs a focused configured linter set.
